# Welcome {.unnumbered}

This is the online book for [**metacheck**](https://scienceverse.github.io/metacheck/), an R package that provides extendable and integrated tools for automated checks of research outputs for best practices.

Inspired by practices in software development — where automated checks (e.g., CRAN checks for R packages) identify issues before release — metacheck screens scientific manuscripts to flag potential issues or areas for improvement, and guides researchers in adopting best practices. It can also assist with processing large numbers of papers for metascientific enquiry.

These checks are meant to **assist, not replace, human expertise**. metacheck can identify incorrect citations, add missing DOIs, cross-reference citations against Retraction Watch, PubPeer, and the FLoRA replication database, and flag commonly misreported aspects such as power analyses. Actioning this advice is always up to a human who can interpret it in context.

## How this book is organised

- **Getting Started** — installing metacheck and a conceptual overview of what it does and why.
- **Modules** — the building blocks of metacheck: using existing modules, writing your own, and worked examples (statistics, JARS reporting standards).
- **Working with Papers** — reading papers in, batch processing, checking local files, and the paper database corpora.
- **Integrations & Sources** — pulling in data from GitHub, AsPredicted, OSF registration schemas, and running local AI with Ollama.
- **Drafts & Appendices** — work-in-progress material, validation reports, and reference documentation. These chapters are less polished and may not render perfectly.

## Installation

You can install metacheck from [GitHub](https://github.com/scienceverse/metacheck):

``` r
pak::pkg_install("scienceverse/metacheck")
```

## Our Values

The values below guide all Scienceverse projects:

1. **Quality Control** — knowledge, data, code, and software are verified for accuracy using publicly available methods.
2. **Transparency** — open source software and open access datasets for validation.
3. **AI Optional** — LLM use is restricted to classification of existing text, always opt-in and transparently declared, with non-LLM methods prioritised.
4. **No Automated Evaluation** — we will never support automated quality assessments, rankings, or scoring.
5. **Data Privacy** — we are transparent about data access and use, minimise data sharing, and meet all EU data privacy regulations.

You can contact the metacheck team at [metacheck.scienceverse@gmail.com](mailto:metacheck.scienceverse@gmail.com).
